assessments.rationale: Hydronic circuits use water or water-glycol mixtures — exact media match for sensor capability.
Residential/commercial hydronic circuits operate at 40-80 degC. Well within 90 degC limit.
Residential 1-3 bar, commercial 2-6 bar. Safety relief valves cap at 6 bar. Well within 10 bar.
Zone commissioning flows 5-30 l/min. Minimum detectable 4.2 l/min (DN20) or 0.25 l/min (DN14) — below operating range.
Standard commissioning: fill, purge, pressure test, then flow balance. Properly purged closed loops have <0.5% air.
Closed-loop hydronic systems are fully filled and pressurized before commissioning. Partially filled pipes are a fault state caught during pressure testing.
Tight mechanical rooms and retrofit installations may have elbows, tees, or valves close to sensor insertion point. Achievable with design-phase planning or flow straightener.
assessments.mitigation: Specify sensor placement during system design (L6.2) to ensure adequate straight run upstream; Install compact flow straightener upstream of sensor (<$50, adds 2x DN equivalent); Use DN14 variant where space is constrained (shorter body, lower absolute straight-run requirement)

assessments.rationale: HVAC operates in moderate range (5-80 degC) with gradual changes. NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%.
